 {"id":"bd-cuq5","title":"Phase 5: Apply Phase (Quality Gates, gh_actions, Push)","description":"Phase 5: Apply Phase

Overview
--------
Execute approved actions from review plans: run quality gates, perform
GitHub mutations (comments, closes, labels), and push changes.

Why Separate Phase?
-------------------
- Plan mode is safe (no mutations)
- User reviews plans before execution
- Quality gates catch issues before push
- Explicit --apply flag required
- Auditability of what was done

Components
----------

5.1 Quality Gates Framework
   Run tests/lint before allowing push:
   - Per-repo policy configuration
   - Auto-detect test commands
   - Fail blocks push
   - Secret scanning

5.2 Per-Repo Policy Configuration
   ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/owner_repo.conf:
   - REVIEW_TEST_CMD
   - REVIEW_LINT_CMD
   - REVIEW_REQUIRE_TESTS
   - Custom validation scripts

5.3 gh_actions Execution
   Execute actions from review-plan.json:
   - comment: gh issue comment / gh pr comment
   - close: gh issue close / gh pr close
   - label: gh issue edit --add-label
   - (merge not supported - policy says no PRs)

5.4 Worktree Merge and Push
   Merge worktree changes to main:
   - Verify quality gates passed
   - Merge worktree branch to base
   - Push to remote
   - Clean up worktree

5.5 Apply Progress Tracking
   Track what has been applied:
   - Mark items as applied in state
   - Log all gh_actions executed
   - Record push timestamps
   - Enable partial retry

5.6 Rollback Capability
   If something goes wrong:
   - Worktrees preserved until explicit cleanup
   - Can revert commits locally
   - gh_actions not reversible (comments stay)
   - Plan artifact serves as audit log

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Quality gates run before push
- gh_actions execute correctly
- Push only with explicit --push flag
- State tracks what was applied
- Rollback path documented

Estimated Effort

 {"id":"bd-p2ip","title":"Implement review lock mechanism (flock-based)","description":"# Task: Implement Review Lock Mechanism

## Purpose
Prevent concurrent `ru review` runs which would cause chaos with overlapping worktrees, duplicate API calls, and conflicting state updates.

## Implementation Details

### Lock File Location
```bash
REVIEW_LOCK_FILE="$RU_STATE_DIR/review.lock"
```

### acquire_review_lock()
```bash
acquire_review_lock() {
    mkdir -p "$RU_STATE_DIR"
    
    # Open fd 200 for locking
    exec 200>"$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE"
    
    # Try non-blocking lock
    if ! flock -n 200; then
        # Check who holds it
        local holder_info=""
        if [[ -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" ]]; then
            holder_info=$(cat "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info")
        fi
        log_error "Another review session is active"
        [[ -n "$holder_info" ]] && log_error "Started: $holder_info"
        log_info "Use 'ru review --status' to check, or wait for completion"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Write lock info
    cat > "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" << EOF
{
  "run_id": "$REVIEW_RUN_ID",
  "started_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "pid": $$,
  "mode": "$mode"
}
EOF
    
    return 0
}
```

### release_review_lock()
```bash
release_review_lock() {
    # Remove info file
    rm -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info"
    
    # Release flock
    flock -u 200 2>/dev/null || true
}
```

### Trap Handler Integration
```bash
# In cmd_review(), set up trap
trap 'release_review_lock; exit 130' INT TERM

# Ensure cleanup on any exit
trap_cleanup_review() {
    release_review_lock
    cleanup_stale_worktrees
}
trap trap_cleanup_review EXIT
```

### Stale Lock Detection
```bash
check_stale_lock() {
    if [[ -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" ]]; then
        local lock_pid
        lock_pid=$(jq -r '.pid' "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" 2>/dev/null)
        
        # Check if process still exists
        if [[ -n "$lock_pid" ]] && ! kill -0 "$lock_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Found stale lock from dead process $lock_pid"
            rm -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info"
            return 0  # Lock is stale
        fi
    fi
    return 1  # Lock is valid or doesn't exist
}
```

## Why flock?
- Atomic lock acquisition
- Automatically released on process exit (even crash)
- No race conditions
- Works on Linux, macOS, BSD
- Already used successfully in parallel sync

## Edge Cases
- Process killed with SIGKILL: Lock released by kernel, but .info file remains
- Machine reboot: Lock file and .info persist but are stale
- Multiple users: Each user has own state dir, so no conflict

## Testing
- Verify lock prevents second ru review
- Verify lock released on normal exit
- Verify lock released on Ctrl+C
- Verify stale lock detected after kill -9
- Verify lock info shows useful details

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Only one ru review can run at a time
- [ ] Lock info shows run_id, start time, PID
- [ ] Lock released on all exit paths
- [ ] Stale locks from crashed processes handled","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.209434378-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:56:47.476050107-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:56:47.476050107-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented full flock-based review lock mechanism with JSON info file (run_id, started_at, pid, mode). Added check_stale_lock() for dead process detection. Proper trap handlers for INT/TERM/EXIT.  {"id":"bd-xdrt","title":"Phase 3: ntm Integration (Robot Mode, Activity Detection)","description":"Phase 3: ntm Integration

Overview
--------
Integrate with ntm (Named Tmux Manager) robot mode for advanced orchestration
capabilities: activity detection, health monitoring, and question routing.

Why ntm?
--------
ntm provides capabilities beyond basic tmux:
- Velocity-based activity detection (chars/sec)
- Health monitoring with error pattern detection
- Robot mode JSON API for programmatic control
- Workflow pipelines for complex multi-step tasks
- Session management and routing

Components
----------

3.1 ntm Driver Implementation
   Implement the unified driver interface using ntm robot mode API:
   - ntm --robot-spawn: Create sessions
   - ntm --robot-status: Query session state
   - ntm --robot-health: Monitor session health
   - ntm --robot-route: Get best pane for new work
   - ntm --robot-send: Deliver messages to sessions

3.2 Activity State Detection
   ntm detects Claude state via output velocity:
   - GENERATING: velocity > 10 chars/sec (active output)
   - WAITING: velocity < 1 chars/sec + prompt pattern
   - THINKING: low velocity, no prompt
   - STALLED: velocity == 0 for > 30 seconds
   - ERROR: error patterns detected

3.3 Wait Reason Extraction
   When WAITING, determine WHY:
   - ask_user_question: AskUserQuestion tool detected
   - agent_question_text: Question in plain text
   - external_prompt: Git/SSH/auth prompt
   - unknown: At prompt, reason unclear

3.4 Health Monitoring
   Monitor session health continuously:
   - Detect rate limits (429, quota exceeded)
   - Detect crashes (panic, SIGSEGV)
   - Detect stalls (no output for extended period)
   - Trigger recovery actions

3.5 Workflow Pipeline
   Define github-review.yaml workflow:
   - verify_prerequisites: Check gh auth
   - understand_codebase: Create/update digest
   - review_issues_prs: Main review work
   - finalize_artifacts: Validate plan created

3.6 Rate-Limit Governor
   Adaptive concurrency based on real limits:
   - Query GitHub API for remaining quota
   - Detect model rate limits from 429s
   - Adjust parallelism dynamically
   - Circuit breaker for cascading failures

Exit Criteria
-------------
- ntm driver passes all interface tests
- Activity detection works reliably
- Wait reasons extracted correctly
- Health monitoring catches common issues
- Rate-limit governor adjusts concurrency

Estimated Effort
----------------
~400 lines Bash + ~300 lines in ntm (Go)

 {"id":"bd-z89z","title":"Implement atomic state persistence with flock","description":"# Task: Implement Atomic State Persistence

## Purpose
Store review state (outcomes, questions, runs) in JSON files with atomic writes and flock-based locking to prevent corruption from concurrent access or interrupted writes.

## Background: Why Atomic Writes?
- Ctrl+C during write = corrupted JSON
- Concurrent reads during write = partial data
- Power failure = lost state
- Solution: write to temp file, then atomic mv

## State Files

### 1. review-state.json
```json
{
  "version": 2,
  "repos": {
    "owner/repo": {
      "last_review": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
      "last_review_run_id": "abc123",
      "issues_reviewed": 3,
      "prs_reviewed": 1,
      "issues_resolved": 2,
      "outcome": "completed",
      "duration_seconds": 847,
      "digest_hash": "sha256:..."
    }
  },
  "items": {
    "owner/repo#issue-42": {
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "last_review": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
      "outcome": "fixed",
      "notes": "Path handling fixed for Windows"
    }
  },
  "runs": {
    "abc123": {
      "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:00:00Z",
      "completed_at": "2025-01-04T11:30:00Z",
      "repos_processed": 8,
      "items_processed": 14,
      "questions_asked": 12,
      "mode": "ntm"
    }
  }
}
```

### 2. review-questions.json
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q_abc123",
      "run_id": "run_xyz",
      "repo": "owner/repo",
      "session_id": "ru-review-owner-repo",
      "pane_id": "1",
      "context": "Should I refactor...",
      "options": ["a) Minimal fix", "b) Full refactor"],
      "priority": "normal",
      "detected_at": "2025-01-04T10:45:00Z",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Implementation

### State Lock
```bash
# Global state lock (separate from review session lock)
STATE_LOCK_FD=201

acquire_state_lock() {
    local lock_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/state.lock"
    exec 201>"$lock_file"
    flock -x 201
}

release_state_lock() {
    flock -u 201 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Scoped lock helper
with_state_lock() {
    acquire_state_lock
    "$@"
    local rc=$?
    release_state_lock
    return $rc
}
```

### Atomic JSON Write
```bash
write_json_atomic() {
    local file="$1"
    local content="$2"
    local tmp_file="${file}.tmp.$$"
    
    # Write to temp file
    echo "$content" > "$tmp_file"
    
    # Validate JSON before committing
    if ! jq empty "$tmp_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        rm -f "$tmp_file"
        log_error "Invalid JSON, refusing to write: $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Atomic move
    mv "$tmp_file" "$file"
}
```

### Read with Lock
```bash
read_state_json() {
    local file="$1"
    local default="${2:-{}}"
    
    acquire_state_lock
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        cat "$file"
    else
        echo "$default"
    fi
    release_state_lock
}
```

### Update State
```bash
update_review_state() {
    local updates="$1"  # jq filter
    local state_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/review-state.json"
    
    acquire_state_lock
    
    local current
    if [[ -f "$state_file" ]]; then
        current=$(cat "$state_file")
    else
        current='{"version":2,"repos":{},"items":{},"runs":{}}'
    fi
    
    local updated
    updated=$(echo "$current" | jq "$updates")
    
    write_json_atomic "$state_file" "$updated"
    
    release_state_lock
}
```

### Record Item Outcome
```bash
record_item_outcome() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local item_type="$2"
    local number="$3"
    local outcome="$4"
    local notes="$5"
    
    local item_key="${repo_id}#${item_type}-${number}"
    local now
    now=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    
    update_review_state "
        .items[\"$item_key\"] = {
            \"type\": \"$item_type\",
            \"number\": $number,
            \"last_review\": \"$now\",
            \"outcome\": \"$outcome\",
            \"notes\": $(echo "$notes" | jq -R .)
        }
    "
}
```

## Checkpoint State (for Resume)
```bash
checkpoint_review_state() {
    local checkpoint_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/review-checkpoint.json"
    
    local state
    state=$(cat << EOF
{
  "version": 1,
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "run_id": "$REVIEW_RUN_ID",
  "mode": "$REVIEW_MODE",
  "repos_total": ${#REVIEW_REPOS[@]},
  "repos_completed": ${#COMPLETED_REPOS[@]},
  "repos_pending": $(printf '%s\n' "${PENDING_REPOS[@]}" | jq -R . | jq -s .),
  "questions_pending": $(cat "$RU_STATE_DIR/review-questions.json" 2>/dev/null || echo '{"questions":[]}')
}
EOF
)
    
    with_state_lock write_json_atomic "$checkpoint_file" "$state"
}
```

## Cleanup Old State
```bash
cleanup_old_review_state() {
    local max_age_days="${1:-30}"
    local state_dir="$RU_STATE_DIR"
    
    # Clean old worktrees
    find "$state_dir/worktrees" -maxdepth 1 -type d -mtime "+$max_age_days" \
        -exec rm -rf {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Prune old runs from state file
    local cutoff
    cutoff=$(date -u -d "$max_age_days days ago" +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ 2>/dev/null || \
             date -u -v-${max_age_days}d +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    
    update_review_state "
        .runs |= with_entries(select(.value.started_at > \"$cutoff\"))
    "
}
```

## Testing
- Write, crash, verify temp file cleaned up
- Concurrent writes don't corrupt
- Read during write gets consistent data
- Checkpoint and resume works
- Malformed JSON rejected

+test_run_secret_scan_detects_potential_secret() {
+    local test_name="run_secret_scan: detects potential secret patterns"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    require_jq_or_skip || return 0
+
+    # Skip if gitleaks is installed (it may have different behavior)
+    if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
+        skip_test "gitleaks installed, testing regex fallback"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    local env_root
+    env_root=$(create_test_env)
+    local project_dir="$env_root/project"
+
+    mkdir -p "$project_dir"
+    git -C "$project_dir" init -q
+    echo "Clean initial" > "$project_dir/README.md"
+    git -C "$project_dir" add .
+    git -C "$project_dir" commit -q -m "Initial"
+
+    # Add a file with potential secret pattern
+    echo 'API_KEY = "sk-abc123"' > "$project_dir/config.txt"
+    git -C "$project_dir" add .
+
+    local result
+    result=$(run_secret_scan "$project_dir" "staged")
+    local rc=$?
+
+    # Should return 2 (warning) for regex fallback
+    assert_equals 2 "$rc" "Should return warning code for potential secret"
+
+    local warning findings_count
+    warning=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.warning')
+    findings_count=$(echo "$result" | jq '.findings | length')
+
+    assert_equals "true" "$warning" "warning should be true"
+    if [[ "$findings_count" -gt 0 ]]; then
+        pass "Should have findings"
+    else
+        fail "Should have findings"
+    fi
+
+    cleanup_temp_dirs
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
